decisions.decision: Three CLAUDE.md layers: root CLAUDE.md (host project, skip-if-exists), .claude/CLAUDE.md (cnogo shell, always overwritten), and language templates (docs/templates/CLAUDE-*.md)
decisions.rationale: cnogo is installed into other projects. .claude/CLAUDE.md is the shell — always-on context loaded alongside the host project's own CLAUDE.md. This is the correct home for Operating Principles.

decisions.decision: Remove all 'Karpathy' branding; call them 'Operating Principles'
decisions.rationale: User directive: principles are generic best practices, not attributed to a single person. Remove GitHub link attribution.

decisions.decision: Principles enforced at coding/implementation time via .claude/CLAUDE.md (always-on) and /implement preamble, NOT as review-time checklist
decisions.rationale: .claude/CLAUDE.md is always overwritten on install and always loaded by Claude — this is the coding-time enforcement point. Review should verify outcomes, not re-check intent.

decisions.decision: Review restructured around security, performance, and design pattern alignment
decisions.rationale: User directive: review should catch real defects (security holes, perf regressions, pattern violations), not re-validate coding principles

decisions.decision: Principles become optional notes in REVIEW.json, not a mandatory checklist
decisions.rationale: Some principles may be highlighted during review (e.g. 'Simplicity First' if over-engineering detected) but not as a required section

decisions.decision: Rename WORKFLOW.json enforcement.karpathyChecklist to enforcement.operatingPrinciples; remove reviewPrinciples array (principles no longer review-scoped)
decisions.rationale: Config key should reflect the new name. reviewPrinciples array was review-time enforcement — no longer needed since principles are coding-time via CLAUDE.md

decisions.decision: REVIEW.json: replace mandatory principles[] with securityFindings[], performanceFindings[], patternCompliance[], and optional principleNotes[]
decisions.rationale: Review artifacts should reflect what review actually checks: security, performance, patterns

decisions.decision: REVIEW.md: replace '## Karpathy Checklist' with '## Security', '## Performance', '## Design Patterns' sections
decisions.rationale: Human-readable review should mirror the structured JSON review focus areas

decisions.decision: Update WORKFLOW-TEMPLATE.json and all CLAUDE-*.md templates to use new naming
decisions.rationale: New installs must get the updated config keys and principle naming from day one

constraints: Python stdlib only — no external dependencies
Backward-compatible: existing REVIEW.json with principles[] must still parse (warn, not error)
WORKFLOW.json must accept both old key (karpathyChecklist) and new key (operatingPrinciples) during migration
All 8 existing principles remain as Operating Principles, just renamed and relocated
.claude/CLAUDE.md is always overwritten on install — safe to modify without migration concerns
Root CLAUDE.md templates (docs/templates/CLAUDE-*.md) must also be updated for new installs
